title: [espeon] Battle Guide

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[espeon] is a single-type [psychic] special attacker whose Champions role hinges entirely on its hidden ability [magic-bounce]. With 130 SpA and 110 Speed, it sits in the top offensive lead tier, and the whole game plan is to reflect the opponent's stealth-rock, sticky-web, thunder-wave, and toxic back in their face on turn one for a clean tempo lead.

## Strengths
[magic-bounce] is one of the strongest abilities in the current meta — entire hazard and status leads fold the moment [espeon] comes in. Off 130 SpA, [psychic] two-shots neutral Fighting, Poison, and Bug pivots, and [dazzling-gleam] is the second STAB that punishes Dark and Dragon switch-ins. Its 110 Speed tier outruns most leads and mid-speed attackers, locking in first-turn damage on the exchange.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Mono-[psychic] carries three bad weaknesses (Dark, Ghost, Bug), and sucker-punch or shadow-sneak almost always trade [espeon] for one kill. Its 65/60 physical bulk is glass — entering against a physical Dark or Ghost attacker is simply a loss. Tera timing is load-bearing: missing the window means [espeon] goes down without converting the reflection value.

## Recommended Moveset
[psychic] is the locked [psychic] STAB that breaks Fighting and Poison walls. [dazzling-gleam] is [fairy] coverage for Dark and Dragon. [shadow-ball] cleanly wins the mirror match against opposing [espeon] and Ghost pivots, and the last slot is [calm-mind] so a turn of reflected hazards can convert into a late-game sweep. Build-dependent alternatives include grass-knot for Ground cores or morning-sun for longevity.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default because it guarantees one survived priority hit and keeps the [magic-bounce] loop alive for another turn. [twisted-spoon] is the damage upgrade that flips two-shot lines into one-shots on the damage roll. [choice-scarf] is the match-up-specific option when the opposing team stacks scarfers. The ability must be [magic-bounce], and Tera [psychic] maxes [psychic] while Tera [fairy] flips the Dark weakness.

## Teammates & Synergy
Because [espeon] is frail, healing-wish support and U-turn pivots make every clean re-entry matter. [espeon] protects your own stealth-rock setter by reflecting opposing hazards before they land, which is a free bonus for the team. A [fairy] or Fighting answer to Dark and Ghost physical attackers is mandatory behind [espeon] on every build.

## Play Patterns
Lead [espeon] into any opposing hazard or status setter, reflect, and immediately trade for chip damage. Against predictable setup sweepers, use [calm-mind] behind the reflection turn to build a late-game win condition. Tera the instant a Dark or Ghost priority attack is telegraphed — the swap is the entire survival plan.
